Day 3 of 5 — built and shipped in one evening. Sources retrieved July 16, 2026.
FIVE DAYS OF FABLE 5 · DAY 3
The Farm — Jeff's, not Clarkson's.
A simple Vercel app, built on the Twitter / X / Threads playbook.
One evening. One phone call. One deploy. Here's how Day 3 actually happened.
THE CHALLENGE
Five days. Five builds. One model.
Fable 5 in the driver's seat, shipping something real every day. Day 3 needed a spark — and it came from an unexpected phone call.
THE PIVOT
We almost led with a Google Sheets extension.
Useful? Sure. Exciting? About as thrilling as a spreadsheet sounds. Then the phone rang.
THE CALL
Jeff didn't want another template.
My friend Jeff Von Stetton of Holistics Unlimited [1] called. He wanted something that belonged to his community — not a generic platform — and asked if I wanted to collaborate.
I'VE BUILT THIS BEFORE
I used to run production for Piczo.
The social network for the MySpace-to-Facebook generation, founded by Jim Conning [2]. Standing up a community platform isn't new ground for me.
THE BLUEPRINT
Twitter's playbook, re-rooted.
A feed, posts, photos and video, real sign-in — the X / Threads pattern, rebuilt for regenerative agriculture and honest wellness brands.
SHIPPED MID-CALL
I sent Jeff the prototype before we hung up.
Not a mockup. A working app he could click through while we were still on the line.
THE LAUNCH
Deployed to Vercel the same night.
No pitch deck. No six-month roadmap. From a phone call to a live site in a matter of hours — and the whole build is open source [3].
DAY 3, DONE
This is just the first seed.
Thanks to Jeff Von Stetton for the spark, and Jim Conning for writing the original playbook. Two more days of Fable 5 to go. Full walkthrough on video [4].
